meta.name: Organic Fertilizer Lookup for Low NPK
meta.purpose: Return two natural fertilizer options when soil N, P, or K is low
meta.note: Approximate costs vary by city, scale, and whether the fertilizer is homemade or purchased.

recommendations.name: Vermicompost
recommendations.why: Improves nitrogen availability and soil structure.

recommendations.name: Cow dung compost / manure
recommendations.why: Adds organic matter and slow-release nitrogen.

recommendations.name: Bone meal
recommendations.why: Rich in phosphorus and calcium for root growth.

recommendations.name: Rock phosphate
recommendations.why: Slow-release phosphorus source for long-term soil improvement.

recommendations.name: Wood ash
recommendations.why: Contains potassium and some phosphorus/calcium; use only clean wood ash.

recommendations.name: Neem cake
recommendations.why: Organic input with some NPK and natural pest-suppressing value.

safety_and_usage_notes: Use wood ash carefully on alkaline soils because it can raise pH.
Do not mix wood ash directly with urea or ammonium-based fertilizers.
Always verify soil test values before applying any amendment.
These are advisory suggestions, not a replacement for district agriculture guidance.
